scope.summary: Implement keyframeable Trim Weight (trim_weight_scale track) in MaterialAnimationTracks and wire it through MaterialFrameParams per-frame upload to match MD 2025.2 keyframeable Trim Weight
acceptance_criteria: MaterialFrameParams struct in tailor-solver/src/params.rs MUST include trim_weight_scale: f32 (multiplier on GpuTrimBody inv_mass; 1.0 = nominal) matching the canonical extension in spec TR-7.3
MaterialAnimationTracks struct MUST include a trim_weight_scale: KeyframeTrack<f32> field alongside the existing pressure/solidify/shrink tracks
The animation sim-over-timeline loop MUST compute trim_weight_scale from the keyframe track at each frame and include it in the MaterialFrameParams uploaded per substep
GpuSimParams or equivalent per-frame uniform MUST carry trim_weight_scale so the WGSL trim-rigid pass can scale GpuTrimBody inv_mass without a full buffer re-upload
TailorSimRunCompleted EventLedger payload MUST include the MaterialFrameParams array (including trim_weight_scale values) for full animation reproducibility per TR-7.4
The tailor_animations.animation_json column MUST persist trim_weight_scale track data so it survives session boundaries
The ANIMATION_STABLE ValidationDescriptor MUST check for NaN/Inf in trim_weight_scale values across the frame range

A drape-plus-animation integration test MUST assert that a button with trim_weight_scale animated from 1.0 to 3.0 over 30 frames produces measurably different cloth displacement compared to a static trim_weight_scale=1.0 run
scope.risk_if_missed: MD 2025.2 trim weight keyframing enables animated physics effects like a coat getting heavier as it fills with water or a weighted hem swinging. Without it Tailor cannot reproduce this MD interaction mode, breaking parity with a 2025.2 release feature that real production workflows use. The spec (TR-7.3) explicitly requires trim_weight_scale in MaterialFrameParams; leaving MT-183 as the sole coverage means static-mass-only trim simulation and a gap the ModelManual cannot paper over.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
